Jan. 8 (UPI) --French PresidentEmmanuel Macronannounced on Monday that he had accepted the resignation of the country's prime minister.Prime MinisterElisabeth Borne-- who wasappointed to the jobin May 2022 after Macron won his second term -- is only the second woman prime minister to lead the French government.News of the decision about her job comes as a government reshuffling is expected later this summer amid June's European Union parliamentary elections and the challenges they could present for Macron.Her successor was not named.Macron gave thanks to Borne, 62, for her "examplary" service to France as head of theCouncil of Ministers."You have put our project into effect with the courage, engagement and the determination of a stateswoman," Macronposted on X, formerly known as Twitter.The JuneEU parliamentary electioncomes at a time when France is months away from global attention on the nation as it hosts the2024 Olympic Games. It also comes after the 2022 election defeat by Macron's party, which resulted in the loss of a majority in the lower House of the French parliament. That, in turn, forced Borne to work closely with opposition forces, whichsent approval ratingsdown.Amongthe possible replacementsfor Borne's post are Education MinisterGabriel Attal-- the rumored frontrunner who would be France's youngest and the first openly gay prime minister -- or Armed Forces MinisterSebastien Lecornuor ex-Agriculture MinisterJulien Denormandie.Read MoreFrance's Jan. 8 (UPI) --French PresidentEmmanuel Macronannounced on Monday that he had accepted the resignation of the country's prime minister.

Prime MinisterElisabeth Borne-- who wasappointed to the jobin May 2022 after Macron won his second term -- is only the second woman prime minister to lead the French government.

News of the decision about her job comes as a government reshuffling is expected later this summer amid June's European Union parliamentary elections and the challenges they could present for Macron.

Her successor was not named.

Macron gave thanks to Borne, 62, for her "examplary" service to France as head of theCouncil of Ministers.

"You have put our project into effect with the courage, engagement and the determination of a stateswoman," Macronposted on X, formerly known as Twitter.

The JuneEU parliamentary electioncomes at a time when France is months away from global attention on the nation as it hosts the2024 Olympic Games. It also comes after the 2022 election defeat by Macron's party, which resulted in the loss of a majority in the lower House of the French parliament. That, in turn, forced Borne to work closely with opposition forces, whichsent approval ratingsdown.

Amongthe possible replacementsfor Borne's post are Education MinisterGabriel Attal-- the rumored frontrunner who would be France's youngest and the first openly gay prime minister -- or Armed Forces MinisterSebastien Lecornuor ex-Agriculture MinisterJulien Denormandie.
title: French prime minister resigns ahead of expected government reshuffle - UPI.com
description: Reports of a government reshuffle in France are expected to happen amid the European Union's parliamentary elections scheduled for June.
keywords: Top News, World, Politics, Emmanuel Macron
